prompt:
You are the GREEN TEAM resuming after a Foundry spec_update_and_restart event.

You are producing a self-contained implementation artifact for audit. Use only the revised NLSpec How section and opaque outcome labels. You must not inspect or refer to red tests, human-readable test names, assertions, expected values, raw failure output, red reasoning, or NLSpec Done criteria.

Revised NLSpec How section: Normalize input before separator handling by transliterating accented Latin letters to ASCII base letters where there is an obvious one-letter equivalent (for example é -> e, ü -> u, ã -> a). Trim surrounding whitespace. Lowercase ASCII letters after transliteration. Keep ASCII digits. Treat ASCII whitespace and ASCII punctuation as separators. Treat non-Latin Unicode characters and emoji as separators unless a future NLSpec states a broader transliteration policy. Collapse repeated separators into one hyphen. Strip leading and trailing hyphens. Return untitled when no ASCII letters or digits remain after normalization.

Test results:
  T-001: FAIL
  T-002: FAIL
  T-003: FAIL
  T-004: PASS

## Task
Return JSON only with this exact shape:
{
  "post_restart_green_implementation": {
    "path": "resumed/green/src/lib.rs",
    "rust_code": "complete Rust source code defining pub fn slugify(input: &str) -> String"
  },
  "barrier_confirmation": "string"
}
Do not mention or use any file path outside this run artifact. Do not infer or request human-readable test names.

visible_context.content: Normalize input before separator handling by transliterating accented Latin letters to ASCII base letters where there is an obvious one-letter equivalent (for example é -> e, ü -> u, ã -> a). Trim surrounding whitespace. Lowercase ASCII letters after transliteration. Keep ASCII digits. Treat ASCII whitespace and ASCII punctuation as separators. Treat non-Latin Unicode characters and emoji as separators unless a future NLSpec states a broader transliteration policy. Collapse repeated separators into one hyphen. Strip leading and trailing hyphens. Return untitled when no ASCII letters or digits remain after normalization.
